title: tradescantia edwardsiana
intro: Tradescantia edwardsiana, also known as the Moses-in-the-cradle plant, is a low-growing, spreading perennial native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. Tradescantia edwardsiana has been reported to have health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in the healing of wounds. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be prepared by drying the leaves and roots of the plant and then steeping them in hot water.
